title: Digital Zeitgebers: Environmental Cues for Cognitive Regulation
abstract: Technology-based timing cues for circadian and cognitive support

body.text: This paper examines digital zeitgebers as environmental cues for cognitive prosthetics, exploring how technology-based timing signals can support circadian regulation, cognitive timing, and daily rhythmicity for neurodivergent individuals.

Zeitgebers are environmental cues that synchronize biological rhythms. In the digital age, we can create artificial zeitgebers through technology to support cognitive regulation and daily functioning.
P31 Labs explores integrating customizable digital zeitgebers into artifacts like Node Zero to support circadian regulation, task timing, and cognitive rhythm management for neurodivergent users.
